own_review.issue: 'server side events' is incorrect terminology — SSE stands for 'Server-Sent Events'; this is user-facing metadata shown on code.quarkus/Maven Central
New yaml files omit the top-level 'description:' field present in core/runtime's quarkus-extension.yaml; works via inherited pom <description> but inconsistent with project convention
Description placed on the parent (packaging=pom) module is inherited by deployment and integration-tests artifacts too; stdio keeps a blank line after description while sse removed it

points.note: Verified in diff; matches human point 3

points.point: Both new yaml files omit top-level description field present in core/runtime yaml
points.note: Verified: core yaml line 2 has description, new files do not; relevant since the PR's whole purpose is correct descriptions

points.point: Guide URL active in new files but commented out in core yaml; confirm docs are live
points.note: Verified: core yaml line 6 has the guide commented out with a publishing note; human reviewer also engaged on the guide URL lines ('This guide is already published')

missed: 'server side events' should be 'Server-Sent Events' in sse pom description and yaml name (judge's own finding)
exceeds: Missing yaml description-field consistency point (not in human review)

points.point: Both new yaml files omit top-level description field; suggests concrete description text per transport
points.note: Verified against core/runtime yaml; includes actionable suggested wording

points.point: Guide URL uncommented here vs commented in core with publishing note; confirm URL is live
points.note: Verified: core yaml line 6 matches the described commented-out guide; aligns with human's guide-URL comments on both yaml files

missed: 'server side events' should be 'Server-Sent Events' in sse pom description and yaml name (judge's own finding)
exceeds: Missing yaml description-field consistency point with concrete suggested text (not in human review)

qualitative: This is a tiny metadata-only PR, and both candidates reviewed it accurately: each caught the 'stanard' typo the human flagged, raised the guide-URL question the human also engaged on, and exceeded the human by spotting the missing top-level description field versus the core extension's yaml convention. Every factual claim about the core yaml (description present, guide commented out with a publishing note) was verified true against the repo, so neither candidate has fabrications. Both missed the terminology error that SSE means 'Server-Sent Events', not 'server side events', in user-facing metadata. v6 is marginally stronger in presentation — precise line references and concrete suggested description strings — but substantively the two reviews are equivalent, hence identical nets.
